C[enjoycarrots: I apologize. I think it's still possible for black to win if the rest of endgame goes well, but any love I have for playing this game has died. I've lost every game in this round of the tournament by resignation because I hadn't played any go since the last round of this tournament ended. When I'm far enough behind that I can't win honestly, I resign. When my opponents are far enough behind that they can't win honestly, they drag the game out until the very last move and I end up resigning because I stop caring and make a stupid mistake. There is no fun here for me anymore, and there hasn't been for a long time.
enjoycarrots: AI thinks I have almost no chance of winning, but it's within 4 points, so whithin range with minor endgame mistakes, at least my assessment of the game wasn't too far off
komu: Thanks for the game!Yeah, I thought that I had a small lead, but wasn't sure I can convert it to a win. Anywa

komu: Anyway, it was nice playing with you. I've also played very few games recently, basically just tournament games. And it's certainly not as fun to play these as real-life games. You'd think it's easier to concentrate when there's no time limit, but it's not since you have to reload the game into your head everytime when you come back..

komu: If black plays the atari first, white can play L19 instead of connecting and gets a ko. But white even has time to remove black's only threat on the board.

komu: Interesting position though, perhaps just playing the good shape of J15 instead of J16 as white would have been better. Black can't save H16 cutting stone after that and the aji is better for white. Black can't really squeeze white in that position. Now in the game black gets some sente moves outside.

komu: Well, I guess doing tsumego and reviewing your games (with stronger player if possible,  but also by yourself) are the most important things. Personally I've read quite a lot of books, studied pro games and had a 6 dan teacher for some time. But daily tsumego (both easy and harder problems and no peeking at the answers) and playing/reviewing are probably most important.
komu: If you wish, I can review our game.

C[Lord_o_o_Spoon: Hi there, have a great game.
C[Lord_o_o_Spoon: Just noticed you ranked up... well done my dude.
C[komu: Thanks, it goes back and forth. Let's see if it sticks around this time. :)
C[Lord_o_o_Spoon: i know that feeling XD. here's to hoping we can both keep and raise our ranks.

C[komu: Yeah. I got to EGF 1 dan 9 years ago, but  then I stopped playing for a long time. Now I'm trying to get back into the game and learn all the new developments that have happened since. And really focus back on the basics this time.
C[komu: By the way, a couple of other kyu-players in the tournament asked for me to review the games afterwards. Would you like me to review this one as well?

Lord_o_o_Spoon: if you would not mind doing a review i would love to have one from you.  I know another player that is around one dan (BGA) but he does not play to much. But ive taken breaks from go myself, its alwayus difficult getting back in to the swing of things.
C[Lord_o_o_Spoon: Shall we call it here? There is only small endgae left, and i think we can both see who has won the game.
C[Lord_o_o_Spoon: Thank you very much for the game. I think i learned a few things. you played very well.
komu: Thanks for the game as well! I'll make a review of the game in the evening.
komu: ...and of course there is also the full Leela review for this game as I'm a supporter
komu: It should also be available to you, I think. But of course it's a bit hard to analyze.

timuzhti: White has 12 (LL) + 15 (TL) + 12 (TR) + 8 (R) + komi and some aji.

timuzhti: Though it's probably obvious to most people that ""this whole fight"" is the key area.
